title: Caregiver Stress Self-Check
summary: 5 minutes · An honest check-in, not a diagnosis

questions.question: How often do you feel exhausted, even after a full night's sleep?
How often do you feel resentful or short-tempered about caregiving tasks?
How much has caregiving crowded out your own sleep, meals, or health needs?
How connected do you still feel to friends, hobbies, or things outside of caregiving?
How often do you feel like there's no one else who can step in and help?
Have you had thoughts that you can't keep doing this, or that something has to change?

results.title: You're carrying a real load, and handling it
results.body: Your answers suggest caregiving is demanding, but you still have some room to breathe. This is a good time to build in small supports before things get heavier.

results.title: Some signs of strain are showing up
results.body: Based on your answers, caregiving is starting to take a real toll — on your sleep, your patience, or your own life outside of it. Worth addressing now, before it builds further.

results.title: What you're feeling is real, and it matters
results.body: Your answers point to real burnout signs — exhaustion, isolation, or feeling like there's no way out. This deserves attention, and you deserve support, not just for your loved one's sake but for your own.

nextSteps: Read "Caregiver Burnout: Recognizing It Before It Recognizes You" for concrete next steps
Ask for concrete help with one specific task this week, not "everything"
Talk to your own doctor if exhaustion, sleep, or mood concerns feel like more than you can manage alone
Check back in with this self-check periodically — burnout builds gradually
disclaimer: This self-check is informational only and isn't a mental health diagnosis. If you're having thoughts of self-harm or feel unable to cope, please reach out to a crisis line or a mental health professional.
